name: Siskiyou
description_short: Siskiyou Credit Union is a member-owned NCUA-insured credit union serving Siskiyou County residents with savings accounts, loans, and community-focused banking services across two branches.
description_long:
Siskiyou Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union serving the Siskiyou County community in Northern California. The institution has established itself as a community-first financial institution with roots in the local area, emphasizing personalized member service and local decision-making. As of 2025, SCU operates branches in Yreka and Mount Shasta, with the Mount Shasta location recently opened to expand accessibility to South County residents.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of financial products including share savings accounts, money market accounts, club accounts, auto loans, recreational vehicle loans, boat loans, home equity lines of credit (HELOC), and mortgage services. They provide modern digital banking conveniences such as Apple Pay and Samsung Pay integration, mobile apps, access to nearly 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs through shared branching networks, and online home banking services. SCU also offers ancillary services including AutoSMART, TruStage insurance products, identity theft protection, and Get Away Today vacation discounts.

Siskiyou Credit Union distinguishes itself through genuine community commitment and accessibility. Membership is open to anyone who lives, works, attends school, or worships in Siskiyou County, plus local organizations and associations. The credit union emphasizes member education through regular "Scam of the Week" posts on their Facebook page, demonstrating proactive consumer protection efforts. Their career messaging emphasizes helping community members achieve life goals, reflecting a values-driven organizational culture rather than profit maximization.

As a local credit union, SCU offers personalized service and community reinvestment, but members should expect rates that vary based on creditworthiness and market conditions. Current loan rates for new autos range from 6.11%-19.99% APR depending on credit profile, while savings yields are modest (0.06%-0.15% APY), reflecting the competitive landscape. The institution's small size and limited branch network may appeal to those seeking personal relationships with their financial institution, though this may limit convenience compared to larger national banks.
services: Home equity lines of credit - fixed (8.75% APR) and variable (7.25%-8.25% APR)
Digital payment services (Apple Pay, Samsung Pay)
pros: Access to nearly 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs through CO-OP network, eliminating out-of-network fees
Modern digital banking features including Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, and mobile app for convenient transactions
Community-focused membership criteria allowing anyone working, living, or worshiping in Siskiyou County to join
Proactive member education through regular scam awareness posts on social media
Expanded accessibility with new Mount Shasta branch opening in 2025 serving South County residents
Not-for-profit structure means earnings are returned to members rather than shareholders
Full-time employment benefits and career growth opportunities for local job seekers
cons: Savings rates are very low (0.06%-0.15% APY), making accounts uncompetitive for yield-focused savers
Auto loan APR range is broad (6.11%-19.99%), meaning subprime borrowers could face rates near 20%
Limited to two physical branch locations in a rural area may inconvenience some members
No mention of construction loans, business lines of credit, or commercial lending products
Small institution size may mean fewer product innovations compared to larger credit unions or banks
best_for: Siskiyou County residents seeking personalized, community-focused banking with local decision-makers
Borrowers with good-to-excellent credit seeking auto, boat, or RV financing at competitive rates
People who work or worship in Siskiyou County but don't live there, who qualify for membership
Members prioritizing surcharge-free ATM access and digital payment convenience over high savings yields
diagnosis: Siskiyou Credit Union is best for Siskiyou County residents and workers seeking a member-owned alternative to banks with genuine community commitment and surcharge-free ATM access. The main caveat is that savings rates are extremely low and auto loan rates vary dramatically by creditworthiness, so comparison shopping is essential before borrowing.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Siskiyou — Credit Union in Yreka, CA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Siskiyou — NCUA-insured credit union in Yreka, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
